story.iWant: to invite members via email with multi-channel notifications (email + Moku Web + Desktop), manage existing members, and handle invitations
story.soThat: I can collaborate with my team while maintaining proper access control with clear invitation workflows
story.tasks: - Implement Member List Display (avatar, name, email, role, added by) - Implement Invitation Dialog (email input with validation, role selector) - Implement Personal-to-Shared Upgrade Dialog (upgrade confirmation for personal projects) - Implement Invitation Sending (API call, multi-channel notifications) - Implement Pending Invitations Section (show invited emails, expiration, cancel button) - Implement Desktop Notification Center (bell icon, badge count, accept/decline buttons) - Implement Member Removal (confirmation dialog, owner-only, prevent last owner removal) - Implement Role Change (dropdown, owner-only, prevent changing own role if last owner) - Implement Invitation Expiration (7-day TTL, auto-filter expired) - Testing (E2E invitation flow, multi-channel notifications, personal-to-shared upgrade)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Members tab displays list with: name, email, role, "added by" info
"Invite Member" button visible only to owners
Invitation dialog: email input (with validation), role selector (editor/viewer)
Invitation sent: Creates invitation record (status: pending, expiresAt: +7 days)
Multi-channel notifications: Email sent, notification in Moku Web (bell icon), Desktop in-app notification
Desktop notification center shows pending invitations with badge count
Pending invitations section in Members tab shows: invited email, role, invited date, "Cancel" button (owner only)
User can accept/decline invitations from Desktop notification center or email link
Invitation acceptance: Creates member with specified role, updates invitation status to 'accepted'
Invitation decline: Updates invitation status to 'declined', no member created
Invitations expire after 7 days (expiresAt timestamp checked)
"Remove" button visible only to owners, disabled for self if last owner
Role change dropdown visible only to owners
Personal project upgrade: If project.type='personal' and owner tries to add member, show upgrade confirmation dialog; on confirm, upgrade to type='shared' then add member

doc.section: 1. Owner clicks "Invite Member" in Members tab 2. Invitation dialog: Enter email, select role (editor/viewer) 3. API call: POST /api/projects/{id}/invitations 4. Moku API creates invitation (status: pending, expiresAt: +7 days) 5. Multi-channel notifications: Email, Moku Web (bell icon), Desktop in-app 6. New user receives email with link + in-app notifications 7. User accepts: POST /api/invitations/{id}/accept → member created 8. User declines: POST /api/invitations/{id}/decline → status updated
{ id, projectId, invitedEmail, invitedUserId?, role: 'editor'|'viewer', status: 'pending'|'accepted'|'declined', invitedBy, invitedAt, respondedAt?, expiresAt }

1. User clicks "Invite Member" on personal project 2. UI checks project.type field 3. If type='personal': Show UpgradeToSharedDialog 4. User confirms upgrade: POST /api/projects/{id}/upgrade-to-shared 5. API updates project.type='shared' 6. Proceed with invitation dialog 7. Cannot downgrade shared → personal

POST /api/projects/{id}/invitations - Send invitation GET /api/projects/{id}/invitations - List pending invitations (owner only) GET /api/invitations - List user's pending invitations POST /api/invitations/{id}/accept - Accept invitation POST /api/invitations/{id}/decline - Decline invitation DELETE /api/projects/{id}/invitations/{invitationId} - Cancel invitation POST /api/projects/{id}/upgrade-to-shared - Upgrade personal to shared

code.file: Base modal component pattern Used for invitation dialog and upgrade dialog
Will add methods: inviteMember, acceptInvitation, declineInvitation, upgradeToShared IPC calls to backend
Will add IPC handlers for invitation and upgrade operations

constraints.constraint: Remove Member: Owner only (cannot remove self if last owner)
Change Role: Owner only (cannot change own role if last owner)
Multi-channel notifications: Email + Moku Web + Desktop
Personal projects can upgrade to shared, no downgrade

interface.name: MemberManagementUI

method.name: inviteMember
method.signature: inviteMember(projectId: string, email: string, role: 'editor'|'viewer'): Promise<void>

interface.method: Sends invitation via API, triggers multi-channel notifications
Accepts invitation, creates member with specified role

method.name: changeMemberRole
method.signature: changeMemberRole(projectId: string, userId: string, newRole: ProjectMemberRole): Promise<void>

tests.standards: - Unit tests: Email validation, permission checks (owner-only actions) - E2E tests: Full invitation flow (send → email + notifications → accept → member created) - E2E tests: Invitation decline (send → decline → no member created) - E2E tests: Invitation expiration (create, advance time 7 days, verify expired) - E2E tests: Member removal, role change - E2E tests: Personal project upgrade flow (add member → confirm upgrade → verify type changed → member added) - Integration tests: Multi-channel notifications (verify email sent, Moku Web, Desktop) - Performance tests: Member operations <1s (P95)

ideas.idea: Test member list displays all fields: name, email, role, added by
Test "Invite Member" button visible to owner, hidden to editor/viewer
Test invitation dialog validates email format, role selection
E2E: Send invitation → verify email sent + Moku Web notification + Desktop notification
Test notification center shows pending invitations with badge count
Test pending invitations section shows invited email, role, expiration, cancel button
E2E: Accept invitation from Desktop notification center
E2E: Accept invitation → member created with specified role, status='accepted'
E2E: Decline invitation → status='declined', no member created
Test invitation expiration: create, advance 7 days, verify expired and filtered
Test "Remove" button owner-only, disabled for self if last owner
Test role change dropdown owner-only, cannot change own role if last owner
E2E: Personal project upgrade flow (invite on personal → upgrade confirmation → type changed)
